summary.high_unstructured: The code defines three functions to automate scraping of NBA MVP voting results and player statistics from Basketball Reference over a range of seasons. scrape_mvp_votes iterates through each year, downloads the awards table, extracts player names and MVP points, and handles errors if a year's data can't be parsed. scrape_season_stats fetches per-game stats for a given year, removes redundant header rows, selects key columns (PTS, AST, TRB, FG%), and drops rows with missing values. scrape_multiple_seasons loops through the specified years, applies scrape_season_stats to each, and concatenates the results into a single DataFrame for all seasons.
summary.low_structured:
• Functions scrape MVP votes and player stats from Basketball Reference
• Data is collected for multiple NBA seasons
• Results are combined into dataframes
summary.title: Summaries for scrape_mvp_votes, scrape_season_stats, and scrape_multiple_seasons
summary.medium_structured:
• scrape_mvp_votes collects MVP voting data for each year in a range
  ◦ Extracts player names and MVP points from the awards page
• scrape_season_stats retrieves per-game stats for all players in a given year
  ◦ Keeps only points, assists, rebounds, and FG%
• scrape_multiple_seasons aggregates season stats across multiple years
• All data is returned as pandas DataFrames
summary.low_unstructured: These functions scrape NBA MVP votes and player statistics from Basketball Reference for a range of seasons.
summary.medium_unstructured: The provided functions gather NBA MVP voting results and player statistics from Basketball Reference for a specified range of years. They extract, clean, and combine the relevant data into pandas DataFrames. The results include MVP points and key player stats like points, assists, rebounds, and field goal percentage.
summary.high_structured:
• scrape_mvp_votes:
  ◦ Iterates through a range of years, downloading the awards page for each
  ◦ Extracts the first table (MVP voting), adds the year, and appends to a list
  ◦ Handles exceptions by printing a message if parsing fails
  ◦ Concatenates all years' tables, renames columns, and returns only year, player name, and MVP points
• scrape_season_stats:
  ◦ Downloads per-game stats for a given year
  ◦ Removes duplicate header rows and adds the year column
  ◦ Selects only Player, Year, PTS, AST, TRB, and FG% columns
  ◦ Drops rows with missing values in key stats
• scrape_multiple_seasons:
  ◦ Loops through a year range, calling scrape_season_stats for each
  ◦ Aggregates all yearly data into a single DataFrame
• All functions use pandas for data extraction, cleaning, and concatenation

astNodeRef.originalText:
def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]

            all_votes.append(mvp_table)

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

astNodeRef.originalText:
def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]

    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]

    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(

data.instruction:
I would like to add another method scraping the advanced stats from here: https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_2025_advanced.html
including bpm, ws/48, TS%, VORP, WS, PER, and then we would like to merge the scraped results to the output df of scrape_season_stats()

data.updated_code:
def scrape_advanced_stats(year):
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_adv


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Merge with advanced stats
    df_adv = scrape_advanced_stats(year)
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged

  ◦ Handles exceptions by printing a message if parsing fails
  ◦ Concatenates all years' tables, renames columns, and returns only year, player name, and MVP points
• scrape_season_stats:
  ◦ Downloads per-game stats for a given year

  ◦ Aggregates all yearly data into a single DataFrame
• All functions use pandas for data extraction, cleaning, and concatenation

metadata.originalCode:
def scrape_advanced_stats(year):
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_adv


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Merge with advanced stats
    df_adv = scrape_advanced_stats(year)
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Merge with advanced stats
    df_adv = scrape_advanced_stats(year)
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged

def scrape_advanced_stats(year):
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_adv


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Merge with advanced stats
    df_adv = scrape_advanced_stats(year)
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    #mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    #mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

summary.high_unstructured: This code provides functions to scrape advanced and per-game NBA player statistics from Basketball Reference for a specified year, ensuring only valid and relevant columns are retained. It removes repeated header rows, adds the year as a column, and filters out rows with missing key stats. There are two versions of the scrape_season_stats function: one merges advanced and per-game stats, while the other only processes per-game stats, with the latter being used in the multi-season aggregation. The main execution block ensures the output directory exists, then scrapes and saves player stats for seasons 2018 through 2025 as a CSV file.
summary.low_structured:
• Scrapes NBA player stats from Basketball Reference
• Processes multiple seasons
• Saves results to a CSV file
summary.title: Summary of NBA Stats Scraper Functions and Main Execution
summary.medium_structured:
• Defines functions to scrape advanced and per-game NBA stats for a specified year
• Processes and cleans the data by removing repeated headers and invalid entries
• Handles multiple seasons by aggregating yearly data
• Saves the final dataset to a CSV file in a 'data' directory
summary.low_unstructured: This code scrapes NBA player statistics from Basketball Reference for multiple seasons and saves them to a CSV file.
summary.medium_unstructured: The code defines functions to scrape both advanced and per-game NBA player statistics for a given year from Basketball Reference. It processes multiple seasons, concatenates the data, and saves the combined stats to a CSV file. The script also includes logic to create a data directory if it doesn't exist.
summary.high_structured:
• Provides functions for scraping NBA player stats from Basketball Reference:
  ◦ scrape_advanced_stats: Fetches advanced stats for a given year, removes repeated headers, adds a year column, keeps only key columns, and drops rows with missing values.
  ◦ scrape_season_stats (two versions):
    ◦ One version merges per-game and advanced stats for a year.
    ◦ The other (used in aggregation) processes only per-game stats, cleans data, and retains essential columns.
• scrape_multiple_seasons aggregates stats across a range of years by calling scrape_season_stats for each year and concatenating the results.
• The main block:
  ◦ Ensures a 'data' directory exists.
  ◦ Initiates scraping for player stats from 2018 to 2025.
  ◦ Saves the aggregated stats as 'season_stats.csv'.
• Includes commented-out code for scraping MVP votes, indicating extensibility.

It removes repeated header rows, adds the year as a column, and filters out rows with missing key stats. There are two versions of the scrape_season_stats function: one merges advanced and per-game stats, while the other only processes per-game stats, with the latter being used in the multi-season aggregation. The main execution block ensures the output directory exists, then scrapes and saves player stats for seasons 2018 through 2025 as a CSV file.

metadata.originalCode:
def scrape_advanced_stats(year):
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_adv


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Merge with advanced stats
    df_adv = scrape_advanced_stats(year)
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    #mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    #mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

data.instruction: There is a minor bug in the main method. I modified scrape_mvp_votes() to scrape_season_stats() and scrape_advanced_stats() but the main method is not updated accordingly. Can you fix that in terms of calling the appropriate method and generating output files?

data.updated_code:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping advanced stats...")
    adv_stats = []
    for year in range(2018, 2026):
        adv_stats.append(scrape_advanced_stats(year))
    adv_df = pd.concat(adv_stats)
    adv_df.to_csv("data/advanced_stats.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Merge with advanced stats
    df_adv = scrape_advanced_stats(year)
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    #mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    #mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

data.selected_code:
import pandas as pd
import os


def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

summary.high_unstructured: This script imports necessary libraries and defines three main functions: one to scrape MVP voting data, one to scrape per-game player statistics for a given year, and one to aggregate stats over multiple seasons. The MVP scraping function collects and renames columns, handling errors if a year's data cannot be parsed, while the stats function filters out invalid rows and ensures only relevant columns are retained. In the main block, the script creates a data directory if needed, scrapes both MVP and player stats for seasons 2018–2025, and saves the results as CSV files. Error handling is included for missing or malformed data, and the script ensures data consistency and cleanliness for downstream analysis.

summary.title: Summary of Basketball Reference Scraper Script
summary.medium_structured:
• Defines functions to scrape MVP votes and player stats from Basketball Reference
• Processes data for seasons from 2018 to 2025
• Organizes and cleans data, keeping only key columns
• Saves MVP votes and season stats as separate CSV files
• Handles missing or malformed data during scraping
summary.low_unstructured: This script scrapes NBA MVP votes and player statistics from Basketball Reference and saves them as CSV files.
summary.medium_unstructured: The code defines functions to scrape NBA MVP voting results and player per-game statistics from Basketball Reference for seasons between 2018 and 2025. It processes the data, cleans it, and saves the results as CSV files in a 'data' directory. The script handles missing data gracefully and ensures only relevant columns are kept.
summary.high_structured:
• Imports pandas and os for data handling and file operations
• Defines scrape_mvp_votes:
  ◦ Iterates through years 2018–2025, scraping MVP voting tables from Basketball Reference
  ◦ Handles errors if a year's data cannot be parsed
  ◦ Renames columns and consolidates results into a DataFrame with only Year, Name, and MVP_Points
• Defines scrape_season_stats:
  ◦ Fetches per-game player stats for a given year
  ◦ Removes repeated header rows and retains only Player, Year, PTS, AST, TRB, FG%
  ◦ Drops rows with missing key stats
• Defines scrape_multiple_seasons to aggregate stats over multiple years
• Main execution block:
  ◦ Creates 'data' directory if it doesn't exist
  ◦ Scrapes MVP votes and saves to 'data/mvp_votes.csv'
  ◦ Scrapes player stats for all seasons and saves to 'data/season_stats.csv'
• Ensures data is cleaned, relevant, and ready for further analysis

mapping.summaryComponent: scrapes NBA MVP votes
player statistics
from Basketball Reference
saves them as CSV files

data.code:
1: import pandas as pd
2: import os
3: 
4: 
5: def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
6:     all_votes = []
7:     for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
8:         url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
9:         tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
10:         try:
11:             mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
12:             mvp_table["Year"] = year
13:             all_votes.append(mvp_table)
14:         except:
15:             print(f"Could not parse year {year}")
16: 
17:     df = pd.concat(all_votes)
18:     df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
19:     return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]
20: 
21: 
22: def scrape_season_stats(year):
23:     base_url_reg = (
24:         f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
25:     )
26:     df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
27:     df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
28:     df_reg["Year"] = year
29: 
30:     df_reg = df_reg[
31:         ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
32:     ]  # Keep only specified columns
33:     df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
34:         subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
35:     )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
36:     return df_reg
37: 
38: 
39: def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
40:     all_stats = []
41:     for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
42:         df = scrape_season_stats(year)
43:         all_stats.append(df)
44:     return pd.concat(all_stats)
45: 
46: 
47: if __name__ == "__main__":
48:     if not os.path.exists("data"):
49:         os.makedirs("data")
50: 
51:     print("Scraping MVP votes...")
52:     mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
53:     mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)
54: 
55:     print("Scraping player stats...")
56:     stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
57:     stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)
58:

data.summary: This script imports necessary libraries and defines three main functions: one to scrape MVP voting data, one to scrape per-game player statistics for a given year, and one to aggregate stats over multiple seasons. The MVP scraping function collects and renames columns, handling errors if a year's data cannot be parsed, while the stats function filters out invalid rows and ensures only relevant columns are retained. In the main block, the script creates a data directory if needed, scrapes both MVP and player stats for seasons 2018–2025, and saves the results as CSV files. Error handling is included for missing or malformed data, and the script ensures data consistency and cleanliness for downstream analysis.

metadata.originalCode:
import pandas as pd
import os


def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

data.updated_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    # Scrape per game stats
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Scrape advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows

    # Keep only Player, TS%, WS/48, BPM
    adv_cols = ["Player", "TS%", "WS/48", "BPM"]
    # Some years may not have all columns, so filter only available ones
    adv_cols_present = [col for col in adv_cols if col in df_adv.columns]
    df_adv = df_adv[adv_cols_present]

    # Merge per game and advanced stats on Player
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on="Player", how="left")

    return df_merged

data.original_code:
import pandas as pd
import os


def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

data.summary: The code defines functions to scrape NBA MVP voting results and both per-game and advanced player statistics from Basketball Reference for seasons between 2018 and 2025. It processes the data, merges per-game and advanced stats, cleans it, and saves the results as CSV files in a 'data' directory. The script handles missing data gracefully and ensures only relevant columns are kept.

summaryData.high_unstructured: This script imports necessary libraries and defines three main functions: one to scrape MVP voting data, one to scrape both per-game and advanced player statistics for a given year (merging them on Player), and one to aggregate stats over multiple seasons. The MVP scraping function collects and renames columns, handling errors if a year's data cannot be parsed, while the stats function filters out invalid rows, ensures only relevant columns are retained, and merges per-game stats with advanced stats. In the main block, the script creates a data directory if needed, scrapes both MVP and player stats for seasons 2018–2025, and saves the results as CSV files. Error handling is included for missing or malformed data, and the script ensures data consistency and cleanliness for downstream analysis.

metadata.originalCode:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    # Scrape per game stats
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Scrape advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows

    # Keep only Player, TS%, WS/48, BPM
    adv_cols = ["Player", "TS%", "WS/48", "BPM"]
    # Some years may not have all columns, so filter only available ones
    adv_cols_present = [col for col in adv_cols if col in df_adv.columns]
    df_adv = df_adv[adv_cols_present]

    # Merge per game and advanced stats on Player
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on="Player", how="left")

    return df_merged

data.selected_code:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

summary.high_unstructured: This code processes basketball MVP data by loading a CSV, selecting key statistical features, and cleaning the data by removing rows with missing values. It splits the dataset into training and testing sets based on years, then trains an XGBoostRanker model using a pairwise ranking objective. The model's predictions for the test years are evaluated using the NDCG metric, and both the predicted scores and ranks are added to the DataFrame for analysis. Finally, the script visualizes the prediction errors for each player in the test years, saving the plots for further review.

The results, including predicted scores and ranks, are printed and plotted for analysis.

• Loads and preprocesses basketball MVP data from a CSV file
  ◦ Selects relevant statistical features and removes rows with missing values
  ◦ Sorts data by year and rank for consistency
• Splits the data into training and testing sets based on years
  ◦ Training: all years except the last two
  ◦ Testing: the most recent two years
• Trains an XGBoostRanker model with specified hyperparameters (pairwise ranking, learning rate, estimators, depth, random seed)
• Predicts rankings for the test set

• Evaluates model performance using the NDCG metric on the test years
• Prints detailed prediction results for test years
• Visualizes and saves bar plots of prediction errors for each player in the test years

evaluates the model's performance using NDCG
visualizes prediction errors for recent years

and plotted for analysis

data.code:
1: import pandas as pd
2: from xgboost import XGBRanker
3: from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
4: import numpy as np
5: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
6: import seaborn as sns
7: 
8: 
9: def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
10:     df = pd.read_csv(path)
11: 
12:     # Features to use
13:     feature_cols = [
14:         "PTS",
15:         "AST",
16:         "TRB",
17:         "FG%",
18:         "BPM",
19:         "WS/48",
20:         "TS%",
21:         "VORP",
22:         "WS",
23:         "PER",
24:     ]
25:     feature_cols = [
26:         col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
27:     ]  # only keep existing features
28:     df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])
29: 
30:     # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
31:     df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])
32: 
33:     X = df[feature_cols]
34:     y = df["Rank"]
35:     groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
36: 
37:     return X, y, groups, df
38: 
39: 
40: def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
41:     scores = []
42:     start = 0
43:     for size in group_sizes:
44:         true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
45:         true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
46:         pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
47:         scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
48:         start += size
49:     return np.mean(scores)
50: 
51: 
52: def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
53:     """
54:     Plot prediction error for a specific year.
55: 
56:     Args:
57:         df: DataFrame containing prediction results
58:         year: Year to plot prediction errors for
59:     """
60:     df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
61:     year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
62:     year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)
63: 
64:     plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
65:     sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
66:     plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
67:     plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
68:     plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
69:     plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
70:     plt.tight_layout()
71:     plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
72: 
73: 
74: def main():
75:     X, y, group, df = load_data()
76: 
77:     # Train-test split on years
78:     unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
79:     train_years, test_years = (
80:         unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
81:         unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
82:     )
83: 
84:     train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
85:     test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)
86: 
87:     X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
88:     y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]
89: 
90:     # Group sizes
91:     train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
92:     test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
93: 
94:     # Model
95:     model = XGBRanker(
96:         objective="rank:pairwise",
97:         learning_rate=0.1,
98:         n_estimators=100,
99:         max_depth=4,
100:         random_state=42,
101:     )
102: 
103:     print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
104:     model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
105: 
106:     # Predict, sort, and evaluate
107:     y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
108:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
109:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
110:         df.loc[test_idx]
111:         .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
112:         .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
113:     )
114: 
115:     ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
116:     print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")
117: 
118:     # Show results
119:     print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
120:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
121:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)
122: 
123: 
124: if __name__ == "__main__":
125:     main()
126:

metadata.originalCode:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators
    n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
    models = {}
    ndcg_scores = {}

    print("Training XGBoostRanker with different n_estimators...")
    for n_est in n_estimators_list:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_est,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        models[n_est] = model
        ndcg_scores[n_est] = ndcg
        print(f"n_estimators={n_est}: NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Select the best n_estimators
    best_n_est = max(ndcg_scores, key=ndcg_scores.get)
    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_est} with NDCG Score: {ndcg_scores[best_n_est]:.4f}")
    model = models[best_n_est]

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate with best model
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"\nFinal Evaluation with best n_estimators ({best_n_est}):")
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":

data.new_code:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators
    n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
    models = {}
    ndcg_scores = {}

    print("Training XGBoostRanker with different n_estimators...")
    for n_est in n_estimators_list:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_est,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        models[n_est] = model
        ndcg_scores[n_est] = ndcg
        print(f"n_estimators={n_est}: NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Select the best n_estimators
    best_n_est = max(ndcg_scores, key=ndcg_scores.get)
    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_est} with NDCG Score: {ndcg_scores[best_n_est]:.4f}")
    model = models[best_n_est]

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate with best model
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"\nFinal Evaluation with best n_estimators ({best_n_est}):")
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

66:     plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
67:     plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
68:     plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
69:     plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
70:     plt.tight_layout()
71:     plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
72: 
73: 
74: def main():
75:     X, y, group, df = load_data()
76: 
77:     # Train-test split on years
78:     unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
79:     train_years, test_years = (
80:         unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
81:         unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
82:     )
83: 
84:     train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
85:     test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)
86: 
87:     X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
88:     y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]
89: 
90:     # Group sizes
91:     train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
92:     test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
93: 
94:     # Try different n_estimators
95:     n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
96:     models = {}
97:     ndcg_scores = {}
98: 
99:     print("Training XGBoostRanker with different n_estimators...")
100:     for n_est in n_estimators_list:
101:         model = XGBRanker(
102:             objective="rank:pairwise",
103:             learning_rate=0.1,
104:             n_estimators=n_est,
105:             max_depth=4,
106:             random_state=42,
107:         )
108:         model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
109:         y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
110:         ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
111:         models[n_est] = model
112:         ndcg_scores[n_est] = ndcg
113:         print(f"n_estimators={n_est}: NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")
114: 
115:     # Select the best n_estimators
116:     best_n_est = max(ndcg_scores, key=ndcg_scores.get)
117:     print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_est} with NDCG Score: {ndcg_scores[best_n_est]:.4f}")
118:     model = models[best_n_est]
119: 
120:     # Predict, sort, and evaluate with best model
121:     y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
122:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
123:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
124:         df.loc[test_idx]
125:         .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
126:         .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
127:     )
128: 
129:     ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
130:     print(f"\nFinal Evaluation with best n_estimators ({best_n_est}):")
131:     print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")
132: 
133:     # Show results
134:     print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
135:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
136:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)
137: 
138: 
139: if __name__ == "__main__":
140:     main()

data.code:
1: import pandas as pd
2: from xgboost import XGBRanker
3: from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
4: import numpy as np
5: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
6: import seaborn as sns
7: 
8: 
9: def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
10:     df = pd.read_csv(path)
11: 
12:     # Features to use
13:     feature_cols = [
14:         "PTS",
15:         "AST",
16:         "TRB",
17:         "FG%",
18:         "BPM",
19:         "WS/48",
20:         "TS%",
21:         "VORP",
22:         "WS",
23:         "PER",
24:     ]
25:     feature_cols = [
26:         col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
27:     ]  # only keep existing features
28:     df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])
29: 
30:     # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
31:     df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])
32: 
33:     X = df[feature_cols]
34:     y = df["Rank"]
35:     groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
36: 
37:     return X, y, groups, df
38: 
39: 
40: def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
41:     scores = []
42:     start = 0
43:     for size in group_sizes:
44:         true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
45:         true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
46:         pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
47:         scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
48:         start += size
49:     return np.mean(scores)
50: 
51: 
52: def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
53:     """
54:     Plot prediction error for a specific year.
55: 
56:     Args:
57:         df: DataFrame containing prediction results
58:         year: Year to plot prediction errors for
59:     """
60:     df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
61:     year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
62:     year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)
63: 
64:     plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
65:     sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
66:     plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
67:     plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
68:     plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
69:     plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
70:     plt.tight_layout()
71:     plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
72: 
73: 
74: def main():
75:     X, y, group, df = load_data()
76: 
77:     # Train-test split on years
78:     unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
79:     train_years, test_years = (
80:         unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
81:         unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
82:     )
83: 
84:     train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
85:     test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)
86: 
87:     X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
88:     y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]
89: 
90:     # Group sizes
91:     train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
92:     test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
93: 
94:     # Try different n_estimators
95:     n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
96:     models = {}
97:     ndcg_scores = {}
98: 
99:     print("Training XGBoostRanker with different n_estimators...")
100:     for n_est in n_estimators_list:
101:         model = XGBRanker(
102:             objective="rank:pairwise",
103:             learning_rate=0.1,
104:             n_estimators=n_est,
105:             max_depth=4,
106:             random_state=42,
107:         )
108:         model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
109:         y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
110:         ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
111:         models[n_est] = model
112:         ndcg_scores[n_est] = ndcg
113:         print(f"n_estimators={n_est}: NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")
114: 
115:     # Select the best n_estimators
116:     best_n_est = max(ndcg_scores, key=ndcg_scores.get)
117:     print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_est} with NDCG Score: {ndcg_scores[best_n_est]:.4f}")
118:     model = models[best_n_est]
119: 
120:     # Predict, sort, and evaluate with best model
121:     y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
122:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
123:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
124:         df.loc[test_idx]
125:         .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
126:         .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
127:     )
128: 
129:     ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
130:     print(f"\nFinal Evaluation with best n_estimators ({best_n_est}):")
131:     print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")
132: 
133:     # Show results
134:     print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
135:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
136:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)
137: 
138: 
139: if __name__ == "__main__":
140:     main()

data.summary: This code processes basketball MVP data by loading a CSV, selecting key statistical features, and cleaning the data by removing rows with missing values. It splits the dataset into training and testing sets based on years, then trains several XGBoostRanker models using a pairwise ranking objective and varying the n_estimators hyperparameter. Each model's predictions for the test years are evaluated using the NDCG metric, with the best-performing model selected for final predictions. Both the predicted scores and ranks from the best model are added to the DataFrame for analysis. Finally, the script visualizes the prediction errors for each player in the test years, saving the plots for further review.

mapping.summaryComponent: Loads and preprocesses basketball MVP data from a CSV file
Selects relevant statistical features and removes rows with missing values
Sorts data by year and rank for consistency
Splits the data into training and testing sets based on years
Training: all years except the last two
Testing: the most recent two years
Trains multiple XGBoostRanker models with different n_estimators values (pairwise ranking, learning rate, estimators, depth, random seed)
Evaluates each model's predictions for the test set using the NDCG metric
Selects the model with the highest NDCG score
Predicts rankings for the test set using the best model
Adds predicted scores and ranks to the DataFrame
Evaluates model performance using the NDCG metric on the test years
Prints detailed prediction results for test years
Visualizes and saves bar plots of prediction errors for each player in the test years

metadata.originalCode:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators
    n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
    models = {}
    ndcg_scores = {}

    print("Training XGBoostRanker with different n_estimators...")
    for n_est in n_estimators_list:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_est,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        models[n_est] = model
        ndcg_scores[n_est] = ndcg
        print(f"n_estimators={n_est}: NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Select the best n_estimators
    best_n_est = max(ndcg_scores, key=ndcg_scores.get)
    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_est} with NDCG Score: {ndcg_scores[best_n_est]:.4f}")
    model = models[best_n_est]

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate with best model
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"\nFinal Evaluation with best n_estimators ({best_n_est}):")
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size

    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model

        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,

    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """

        year: Year to plot prediction errors for

    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (

        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
