import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

data.selected_code:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

summary.high_unstructured: The main() function orchestrates the end-to-end process of training and evaluating an XGBoost ranking model on MVP basketball data. It first loads the dataset and splits it into training and testing sets based on year, with the last two years reserved for testing. The function then trains an XGBRanker model using group information for ranking, predicts scores for the test set, and computes predicted ranks within each year. Model performance is assessed using the NDCG metric, and detailed results, including predicted and true ranks, are printed. Finally, the function generates and saves bar plots visualizing prediction errors for each player in the test years.

metadata.originalCode:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

data.updated_code:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators and pick the best model
    n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_model = None
    best_pred = None

    for n_est in n_estimators_list:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_est,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )
        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_est}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score with n_estimators={n_est}: {ndcg:.4f}")
        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_model = model
            best_pred = y_pred

    # Save the best model
    import joblib
    joblib.dump(best_model, "data/best_xgbranker_model.joblib")
    print("Best model saved to data/best_xgbranker_model.joblib")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate with the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    print(f"Best NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

metadata.originalCode:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators and pick the best model
    n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_model = None
    best_pred = None

    for n_est in n_estimators_list:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_est,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )
        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_est}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score with n_estimators={n_est}: {ndcg:.4f}")
        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_model = model
            best_pred = y_pred

    # Save the best model
    import joblib
    joblib.dump(best_model, "data/best_xgbranker_model.joblib")
    print("Best model saved to data/best_xgbranker_model.joblib")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate with the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    print(f"Best NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

astNodeRef.originalText:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators and pick the best model
    n_estimators_list = [100, 1000, 2000]
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_model = None
    best_pred = None

    for n_est in n_estimators_list:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_est,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )
        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_est}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score with n_estimators={n_est}: {ndcg:.4f}")
        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_model = model
            best_pred = y_pred

    # Save the best model
    import joblib
    joblib.dump(best_model, "data/best_xgbranker_model.joblib")
    print("Best model saved to data/best_xgbranker_model.joblib")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate with the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    print(f"Best NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

data.selected_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

summary.high_unstructured: The plot_ranking_predictions function generates a bar plot to visualize the prediction error (difference between predicted and actual ranks) for each player in a specified year. It first computes the 'RankError' column, filters the DataFrame for the target year, and sorts the data by true rank. The function then creates a seaborn bar plot with player names on the x-axis and prediction errors on the y-axis, using color to distinguish players. Additional formatting includes rotating x-axis labels, adding a horizontal reference line at zero, labeling axes, setting the plot title, and saving the figure as a PNG file in the data directory.
summary.low_structured:
• Plots prediction errors for a given year.
• Saves the plot as an image file.

• Adds plot formatting:

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Grouped bar chart: since each player appears once per year, this is a barplot with hue for distinction
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

    # Grouped bar chart: since each player appears once per year, this is a barplot with hue for distinction
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Grouped bar chart: since each player appears once per year, this is a barplot with hue for distinction
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

data.updated_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot true and predicted ranks for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    # Prepare data for grouped barplot
    plot_df = year_df[["Name", "Rank", "PredictedRank"]].melt(
        id_vars="Name", value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"], var_name="Type", value_name="RankValue"
    )

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankValue", hue="Type", data=plot_df, palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.xlabel("Player Name")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs Predicted Ranks ({year})")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
data.instruction: I would like the Y axis to be rank and group true rank and predicted rank.
data.original_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Grouped bar chart: since each player appears once per year, this is a barplot with hue for distinction
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot true and predicted ranks for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    # Prepare data for grouped barplot
    plot_df = year_df[["Name", "Rank", "PredictedRank"]].melt(
        id_vars="Name", value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"], var_name="Type", value_name="RankValue"
    )

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankValue", hue="Type", data=plot_df, palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.xlabel("Player Name")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs Predicted Ranks ({year})")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
